patientCommunicationExamples.rationale: Communicates professionalism and institutional credibility.

patientCommunicationExamples.title: Explaining audit to patients when relevant
patientCommunicationExamples.example: Sometimes we review how we document and communicate—not to catch people out, but to keep everyone safe and make good practice repeatable.
patientCommunicationExamples.rationale: Frames QA as patient-centred, not bureaucratic.

roleBoundaryNotes.title: Audit is supportive, not punitive
roleBoundaryNotes.note: Present quality work as safety and learning; escalate governance issues through proper channels.

roleBoundaryNotes.title: You do not unilaterally change medical policy
roleBoundaryNotes.note: Identify gaps and route to clinical or operational leads for authorised template or protocol updates.

keyTakeaways: Operational maturity is visible in records and workflows, not only in charm.
Audit mindset strengthens training design and system fixes.
Volume 3 Week 12 QA and leadership themes map here; capstone integration is module 15.
redFlags: Template drift with no ownership for correction
Audit used only to blame front-facing staff while ignoring system design
Missing traceability from communication channel to medical record
Resistance to escalation culture

escalationTriggers.title: Standards gap
escalationTriggers.trigger: Quality or documentation issues risk affecting patient care or governance.
escalationTriggers.action: Escalate through clinic QA pathways.

escalationTriggers.title: Repeated systemic failure signal
escalationTriggers.trigger: Same failure type appears across multiple cases or staff.
escalationTriggers.action: Raise through quality improvement forum; propose structured fix with leadership buy-in.

evidenceTier.summaryNote: QA and audit disciplines are primarily standards and governance driven.

title: Capstone Consultation Pathway Integration
overview: Completes Teaching Manual Volume 3, Week 12 (final competency integration): the capstone should feel like a realistic clinic simulation—intake summary, case concerns, communication choices, handover preparation, and explicit justification for escalation versus watchful waiting. Applied capability, usable documentation, and mature tone matter more than memorised scripts; ethics and scope remain non-negotiable.
learningObjectives: Integrate certificate learning across first contact, structure, screening support, documentation and photography, pre- and post-operative coordination, difficult conversations, and QA thinking
Demonstrate theory connected to scenarios: documentation that is usable not merely long; communication that sounds professional not robotic
Meet programme completion rules including overall pass threshold, mandatory domain performance on ethics, red flags, escalation, documentation, and communication, and faculty-reviewed evidence
Articulate why your capstone choices represent the safest and most professional support response, not only what you would say

body.content:
Volume 3 Week 12 states final assessment should test applied capability. Knowledge must attach to realistic patient scenarios; documentation must help a clinician act; communication should sound mature; ethical judgement and scope awareness stay mandatory.

The manual’s capstone direction: simulate clinic reality with intake summary, case concerns, communication response, handover preparation, and short justification for escalation or non-escalation decisions. Faculty should judge integrated performance: structure, scope, escalation, communication, documentation together.

This programme retains its existing award mechanics (for example overall pass threshold and mandatory domain tags)—your task is to show professional coherence across the whole pathway while remaining a support role, not a substitute clinician.

Volume 3 conclusion: excellent patient-facing care depends on evidence quality, workflow clarity, communication maturity, and timely escalation—Weeks 9–12 bridge academy learning to measurable clinic standards.

Prepare by revisiting weak domains from earlier modules rather than cramming isolated facts.

patientCommunicationExamples.title: Pathway integration language
patientCommunicationExamples.example: Our role is to help your pathway stay clear, safe, and well coordinated while ensuring the clinical team reviews the right information at the right time.
patientCommunicationExamples.rationale: Summarises the consultant academy ethos.

patientCommunicationExamples.title: Capstone-style closure
patientCommunicationExamples.example: Here is what I have captured, here is what still needs the doctor, and here is how we will follow up—so you are never left guessing where you are in the process.
patientCommunicationExamples.rationale: Demonstrates integrated coordination voice.

roleBoundaryNotes.title: Integrated competence still respects scope
roleBoundaryNotes.note: Diploma-level integration does not grant diagnostic, prescribing, or consent authority.

keyTakeaways: Capstone success is judged on integrated safety and professionalism.
Mandatory domains exist because some failures cannot be averaged away.
Volume 3 Week 12 capstone framing maps directly to this module and faculty review.
redFlags: High composite score with failed mandatory ethics or escalation items
Handover that omits uncertainty or red flags to read cleanly
Communication that slips into clinical decision-making or guarantees

escalationTriggers.title: Capstone safety failure
escalationTriggers.trigger: Learner demonstrates unsafe handling of ethics, red flags, or escalation.
escalationTriggers.action: Block progression and route to remediation or faculty review.

evidenceTier.summaryNote: The capstone integrates standards-led educational and governance requirements.

completionRules.description: Receive faculty approval on the capstone pathway task.
